items.text: USED TEXTBOOK SALE! Saturday, May 17, in Room 112, Bryant Hall. Hundreds of titles for biology, history, and economics. Prices range from $3 to $25. Cash only. Sellers, drop off books Friday afternoon. Doors open 9:00 AM. Arrive early for the best selection!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, how much will the books cost?

options.B: A flat fee of fifteen dollars each
options.C: Ten dollars for any single title

questions.explanation: The flyer states prices range from $3 to $25, which the correct option restates as between three and twenty-five dollars.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a buyer who wants the widest choice of books?

options.A: That person should reserve a title online beforehand
options.B: That person should show up close to opening time
options.C: That person should bring a credit card for payment
options.D: That person should sell some used books first

questions.explanation: The flyer advises arriving early for the best selection, implying that coming near the 9:00 AM opening gives the widest choice.

items.text: FREE FLU SHOTS! No appointment needed. Thursday, October 9, from 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM at the Wexler Health Center, second floor. Bring your student ID. Quick and painless. Protect yourself before winter. Walk-ins welcome all day. Stay healthy this season, Wildcats!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what should attendees bring?
questions.explanation: The flyer instructs readers to bring their student ID, which the correct option restates as their student identification card.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about scheduling a visit to the clinic?

options.A: Students must book a slot online
options.C: Visits are limited to morning hours
options.D: Students may arrive without booking ahead

questions.explanation: Because the flyer says no appointment is needed and walk-ins are welcome, it implies students may arrive without booking ahead.

items.text: Hi Marcus, Your replacement student ID is now ready for pickup at the Campus Card Office in Lowell Hall. Please bring $10 in cash to cover the processing fee. The office is open on weekdays until 4:30 PM. Best, Card Services

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Marcus do to receive his new ID?

options.C: Pay a ten-dollar charge in cash

questions.explanation: The email asks Marcus to bring $10 in cash for the processing fee, which the correct option restates as paying a ten-dollar charge in cash.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about visiting the Campus Card Office?

options.A: It accepts visitors throughout the weekend
options.B: It processes requests entirely by mail
options.C: It stays open late into the evening
options.D: It cannot be visited on a Sunday

questions.explanation: Since the office is open only on weekdays until 4:30 PM, it follows that Marcus cannot visit on a Sunday.

items.text: Dear Olivia, Thank you for volunteering for our memory study. Your session is confirmed for Tuesday, June 3, at 2:00 PM in Room 308, Pearson Hall. You will receive $15 in cash afterward. Please arrive ten minutes early. Best regards, Dr. Reyes

questions.stem: According to the email, what will Olivia receive for participating?

options.B: A voucher for the campus store
options.D: A summary of the study results

questions.explanation: The email states Olivia will receive $15 in cash afterward, which the correct option restates as a fifteen-dollar cash payment.

questions.stem: According to the email, when is Olivia's session scheduled?

options.A: On Tuesday afternoon at two o'clock
options.B: On Monday morning at nine o'clock
options.C: On Thursday evening at six o'clock
options.D: On Wednesday afternoon at four o'clock

questions.explanation: The email confirms the session for Tuesday, June 3, at 2:00 PM, which the correct option restates as Tuesday afternoon at two o'clock.

items.text:
Hi Jordan,

A package arrived for you and is being held at the Lincoln Hall mailroom. Please pick it up by Friday, May 9. Bring your student ID. A $5 storage fee applies after that date.

Best,
Maya, Resident Assistant

questions.stem: According to the email, what should Jordan bring to the mailroom?
questions.explanation: The email instructs Jordan to bring a student ID when picking up the package.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about picking up the package on May 10?
questions.explanation: Since a $5 storage fee applies after May 9, picking up on May 10 would mean a charge is added.

items.text: NOTICE: Fire alarms in Bradley Residence Hall will be tested on Tuesday, October 14, from 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM. Residents are advised that alarms will sound intermittently. No evacuation is required during this scheduled testing. Direct questions to the Facilities Office.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when will the testing take place?
questions.explanation: The notice states testing occurs on Tuesday, October 14, from 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM, which is morning.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what should residents do during the testing?
questions.explanation: The notice specifies that no evacuation is required, meaning residents should remain in the building.

items.text: The Rolling Bites truck is parked by the Student Union today until 3 PM, and the tacos are amazing! They're running a special: any taco for just $3. Cash or card accepted. Don't miss it before they roll out!

questions.stem: According to the post, what is being offered for $3?
questions.explanation: The post states the special is any taco for just $3.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about visiting the truck at 4 PM today?
questions.explanation: Since the truck is parked only until 3 PM, it would have left by 4 PM.

items.text: Heads up, everyone! The Tech Commons lab is offering free printing through Friday, May 16. After that, the usual $0.10 per page returns. Great chance to print those final project handouts. Just swipe your ID at the front desk to start!

questions.stem: According to the post, what must students do to begin printing?
questions.explanation: The post says to swipe your ID at the front desk to start printing.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about printing on Saturday, May 17?
questions.explanation: Since free printing ends Friday, May 16, and the $0.10 per page rate returns, printing on May 17 would cost money.

items.text: NOTICE: The Hartwell Library study rooms may now be reserved online. Reservations are limited to two hours per group and must be made at least one day in advance. Rooms not claimed within fifteen minutes will be released. Visit the circulation desk for assistance.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how far in advance must reservations be made?
questions.explanation: The notice states reservations must be made at least one day in advance.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a group arriving twenty minutes late?
questions.explanation: Since rooms not claimed within fifteen minutes are released, a group arriving twenty minutes late would lose their room.

items.text:
Dear Sam,

The writing tutor you requested is available this Wednesday, April 9, at 2:00 PM in Room 215. Sessions last fifty minutes. Please reply to confirm, and bring a draft of your essay. We look forward to seeing you.

Regards,
The Writing Center

questions.stem: According to the email, what is Sam asked to bring?
questions.explanation: The email asks Sam to bring a draft of the essay to the session.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the session if Sam does not reply?
questions.explanation: Since the email asks Sam to reply to confirm, the session might not be held without confirmation.

items.text: USED TEXTBOOK SALE! This Saturday, March 8, from 9 AM to 1 PM in Marshall Hall, Room 100. Hundreds of titles priced from $5. Cash only. Sponsored by the Student Council. Arrive early for the best selection. All proceeds support campus clubs!

questions.stem: According to the advertisement, how may buyers pay?
questions.explanation: The advertisement states the sale is cash only.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about arriving when the sale opens?
questions.explanation: The ad advises arriving early for the best selection, so arriving at opening would mean a better selection.

items.text: NOTICE: South Lot C will be closed for repaving on Monday, June 2, from 7:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Vehicles must be removed by 6:00 AM that day. Permit holders may park in North Lot A at no charge. Contact Campus Parking with questions.

questions.stem: According to the notice, by when must vehicles be removed?
questions.explanation: The notice states vehicles must be removed by 6:00 AM on June 2.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about permit holders using North Lot A that day?
questions.explanation: The notice states permit holders may park in North Lot A at no charge, meaning they would park free.

items.text: NOTICE: During spring break (March 9-15), the Main Library will operate on reduced hours, 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM daily. The third-floor study rooms remain closed. A $5 late fee per day applies to all overdue items returned after March 8.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what happens to items returned after March 8?

options.A: A waiver request must be submitted.
options.C: A daily charge of $5 is added.

questions.explanation: The notice states a $5 late fee per day applies to overdue items returned after March 8, which option C paraphrases as a daily charge of $5.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the library during spring break?

options.A: It stays open fewer hours than usual.
options.B: It moves all materials to another building.
options.C: It charges visitors an entry fee.
options.D: It opens earlier than on regular days.

questions.explanation: The notice describes 'reduced hours' of 10 AM to 4 PM, implying the library is open for a shorter period than it normally would be.

items.text:
Hi Jordan,

A package arrived for you and is being held at the Wexler Hall mailroom. Please collect it by Friday, April 18, between 1:00 and 5:00 PM. Bring your student ID, as we cannot release parcels without it.

Thanks,
Resident Assistant Mia

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Jordan bring to the mailroom?
questions.explanation: The email instructs Jordan to bring a student ID, since parcels cannot be released without it. Option C restates this as a student identification card.

questions.stem: In this email, the word "release" most nearly means to
questions.explanation: Here 'release parcels' means to give the package to the student, so 'hand over to a person' best matches the meaning in context.

items.text:
Dear Members,

This week's Photography Club meeting has been moved. We'll now gather Thursday, May 7, at 6:30 PM instead of 5:00, and we've relocated to Room 312 in Brooks Hall. Please bring your edited photos for review.

Best,
Club President Dev

questions.stem: According to the email, where will the meeting now take place?
questions.explanation: The email states the meeting has relocated to Room 312 in Brooks Hall, which option D directly identifies.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the original meeting plan?

options.A: It was set for an earlier hour.

questions.explanation: The email notes the new time is 6:30 PM 'instead of 5:00,' implying the meeting was originally scheduled for an earlier time.

items.text: ATTENTION: The west elevator in Carter Hall will be out of service for repairs from June 1 through June 5. Residents should use the east stairwell or the freight lift. A $10 deposit is required to borrow the moving cart at the front desk.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is required to borrow the moving cart?
questions.explanation: The notice states a $10 deposit is required to borrow the moving cart at the front desk, which option D paraphrases as a deposit of ten dollars.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about residents during the repair period?

options.A: They must relocate to another building.
options.B: They need another way to reach upper floors.
options.C: They will receive a rent reduction.
options.D: They should report damage to security.

questions.explanation: Since the west elevator is out of service and residents are told to use the stairwell or freight lift, it can be inferred they need an alternative route to upper floors.

items.text: NOTICE: The Student Health Center will close early on Tuesday, October 14, at 2:00 PM for staff training. Walk-in appointments end at 1:30 PM that day. For urgent care after closing, please visit Riverside Clinic on Oak Street. Normal hours resume Wednesday.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when do walk-in appointments end on October 14?
questions.explanation: The notice states walk-in appointments end at 1:30 PM on October 14, matching option B.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students needing care Tuesday evening?

options.A: They should go to an off-campus facility.
options.B: They must wait until the following week.
options.C: They can call the training staff directly.
options.D: They will be charged a higher fee.

questions.explanation: Because the center closes early and directs urgent cases to Riverside Clinic on Oak Street, students needing evening care should visit an off-campus location.

items.text: USED TEXTBOOK SALE! Saturday, September 6, 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM, Student Union Lobby. Science, math, and language titles from $4. Cash only. Sell your old books too—bring them by 8:30 AM for pricing. Sponsored by the Economics Society.

questions.stem: According to the flyer, how can students pay for books?
questions.explanation: The flyer clearly states 'Cash only,' so option A is correct.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who want to sell books?

options.A: They must arrive before the sale opens.
options.B: They will receive a fixed flat rate.
options.C: They need to register online first.
options.D: They can drop books off any time.

questions.explanation: Sellers are told to bring books by 8:30 AM, which is before the 9:00 AM start, implying they must arrive before the sale begins.

items.text: Heads up, everyone! The campus shuttle to Downtown Station is switching to a new schedule starting Monday, November 3. First pickup is now 7:15 AM from Lot C, not 7:45. Fare stays at $2.50. Don't miss the early run!

questions.stem: According to the post, what is the shuttle fare?
questions.explanation: The post states the fare stays at $2.50, which option D expresses as two-fifty.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the new shuttle schedule?

options.A: It begins service earlier than before.
options.B: It adds a second pickup location.
options.C: It raises the cost of a ride.
options.D: It runs less often each day.

questions.explanation: The first pickup moves from 7:45 to 7:15 AM, so the new schedule starts earlier than the previous one.

items.text:
Hi Sam,

Just a reminder that your library study room booking for Room 5B is confirmed for Monday, February 9, from 2:00 to 4:00 PM. If you can't make it, please cancel online by Sunday night so others can use the space.

Best,
Front Desk

questions.stem: According to the email, what should Sam do if unable to attend?

options.B: Call the front desk during hours
options.D: Transfer the room to a friend

questions.explanation: The email instructs Sam to cancel online by Sunday night if he cannot attend, matching option A.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the study rooms?

options.A: They are in high demand among students.
options.C: They are open only on weekdays.
options.D: They must be booked a month ahead.

questions.explanation: The request to cancel so 'others can use the space' implies the rooms are sought after by many students.
